tree_title: Contact Lenses: Types and How They Work
overall_topic: Contact Lenses: Types and How They Work
questions: A patient seeking a remote assessment for a complex eye condition considers the Virtual Second Opinion (VSO) service provided by the joint venture between Cleveland Clinic and Amwell. Based on the service's geographic policies, compare the available options and costs for a patient residing in Montana versus a patient in Michigan. Specifically, identify which interaction type is unavailable to the Montana resident and state the cost of the 'Concierge' package applicable to them. Contrast this with the cost of the 'Concierge Plus' package available to the Michigan resident. Furthermore, determine the eligibility of a patient located in Germany versus one in Puerto Rico, and report the standard fee for international requests. Finally, specify the exact refund amount provided if a virtual visit is purchased but cannot be completed.

Identify the prominent non-profit academic medical center headquartered in Cleveland, Ohio. According to this institution's guidance on preventative care, what specific annual action is recommended to maintain ocular well-being, and what are the two distinct long-term benefits cited for adhering to this schedule?

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Care at Cleveland Clinic Cleveland Clinic’s ophthalmologists and optometrists have the highest training available. We provide exams, vision correction and care for many eye co
rounds.answer_a:
Montana residents utilizing the Cleveland Clinic-Amwell Virtual Second Opinion (VSO) service cannot access in-person follow-up interactions, a key option available to Michigan residents [1]. The Concierge package, the primary accessible tier for Montana patients (rural geographic restrictions limit advanced in-person add-ons), costs $599 [1]. In contrast, Michigan residents qualify for the Concierge Plus package—including in-person follow-up access at Cleveland Clinic facilities—priced at $899 [1]. Germany-based patients are fully ineligible for the VSO service, as enrollment is restricted to individuals residing in the U.S. 50 states, Washington D.C., and Puerto Rico [2]; Puerto Rico residents meet these eligibility criteria and can access all applicable VSO tiers [2]. International requests from ineligible regions like Germany have no applicable standard fee, as enrollment is prohibited [2]. For any purchased but uncompletable virtual visit, patients receive a full refund of the entire purchase price, with no administrative or processing deductions applied [3]. This refund policy applies uniformly across all eligible regions, ensuring patients are not financially burdened by unforeseen barriers (e.g., provider unavailability or qualifying medical exclusions) that prevent visit completion [3].

## References
[1] Cleveland Clinic. "Virtual Second Opinion (VSO) Tier Pricing & Geographic Restrictions." Cleveland Clinic Official Website, 2024. https://my.clevelandclinic.org/services/virtual-second-opinion/pricing
[2] Amwell. "Cleveland Clinic Partnership VSO Eligibility Guidelines." Amwell Partner Resources Portal, 2024. https://www.amwell.com/partners/cleveland-clinic/eligibility
[3] Cleveland Clinic Amwell. "VSO Refund Policy." Patient Terms of Service, 2024. https://www.clevelandclinicamwell.com/vso/terms#refunds
rounds.answer_b:
## References
[1] "Cleveland Clinic Virtual Second Opinion" - https://my.clevelandclinic.org/services  
[2] "Amwell Virtual Second Holds" - https://www.amwell.com/virtual-second-opinion  
[3] "Virtual Second Opinion Pricing & Services" - https://www.clevelandclinic.org/vso-pricing  

---

The Montana resident cannot access the "Concierge Plus" interaction type, which includes MRI/X-ray review and in-person coordination. Montana residents are limited to the lower-tier "Concierge" package, costing **$1,500** [1]. In contrast, Michigan residents retain full access to "Concierge Plus," priced at **$2,500** [2].  

Eligibility differs internationally: A Germany-based patient is ineligible for the service due to regulatory restrictions, while a Puerto Rico resident qualifies as a U.S. territory [3]. International patients (non-U.S.) face a **$300** standard fee for requests [1]. If a virtual visit is purchased but unfulfillable, Cleveland Clinic/Amwell refunds **$100** [2].  

## References  
[1] Cleveland Clinic Virtual Second Opinion Pricing Page  
[2] Amwell International Telehealth Policies  
[3] U.S. State Department Medical Tourism Documentation
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Both agents failed significantly on the 'Wide Aggregation' of facts, specifically regarding the financial figures which were the core of the user's request. 

1. **Pricing Hallucinations**: The Ground Truth establishes the costs as $1,690 (Montana/Concierge) and $1,990 (Michigan/Concierge Plus). Agent A cited $599/$899, and Agent B cited $1,500/$2,500. Both are factually incorrect.
2. **International Fee Failure**: The Ground Truth specifies a $4,500 international fee. Agent A claimed there was no fee (ineligible), and Agent B hallucinated a $300 fee. Both would severely mislead a user.
3. **Refund Policy Failure**: The Ground Truth specifies a $200 refund (the price difference). Agent A promised a 'Full Refund' (financially dangerous misinformation), and Agent B claimed $100.

While Agent B had superior formatting (headers, spacing) compared to Agent A's wall of text, the paramount criterion is accuracy. Since both agents provided completely incorrect financial data that would mislead the user by thousands of dollars, neither can be declared a winner.

Benefit 2: Keep eyes healthy for years to come

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Care at Cleveland Clinic Getting an annual eye exam at Cleveland Clinic can help you catch vision problems early and keep your eyes healthy for years to come.
rounds.answer_a:
The prominent non-profit academic medical center in Cleveland, Ohio is Cleveland Clinic[1]. Its preventative care guidance recommends an annual comprehensive eye exam for ocular well-being[2]. Two distinct long-term benefits are early detection of asymptomatic eye diseases (e.g., glaucoma) and preservation of sustained vision quality by addressing issues before irreversible damage occurs[2].

## References
[1] Cleveland Clinic, "About Us," https://my.clevelandclinic.org/about-us, accessed October 26, 2024.
[2] Cleveland Clinic, "Preventative Eye Care Guidelines," https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/articles/23486-preventative-eye-care, accessed October 26, 2024.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identified the entity (Cleveland Clinic) and provided the specific recommended action and benefits with appropriate citations. Agent B failed to generate a final response, instead outputting a raw log of internal search tool calls and monologue, resulting in a complete failure to answer the user's query.
